channel.title: Posts on Luigi Marini
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Luigi Marini

item.title: Resources for Prospective Politics Students
item.description:
If you are thinking about studying Politics or want to know more about what the subject might look like, the following material can provide you an accessible introduction, in different media, to some of themes and ways in the social and political sciences:

Under construction

Books

  • Acemoglu, Daron, and James A. Robinson. Why Nations Fail: The Origins of Power, Prosperity, and Poverty. London: Profile Books, 2013.
  • Fukuyama, Francis. The Origins of Political Order: From Prehuman Times to the French Revolution. London: Profile Books, 2012.
  • Fukuyama, Francis. Political Order and Political Decay: From the Industrial Revolution to the Globalization of Democracy. London: Profile Books, 2015.
  • Crick, Bernard. Democracy: A Very Short Introduction. Very Short Introductions. Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2002.
  • Miller, David. Political Philosophy: A Very Short Introduction. Very Short Introductions. Very Short Introductions. Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2003.
  • Minogue, Kenneth R. Politics: A Very Short Introduction. Very Short Introductions. Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2000.
  • Mudde, Cas, and Cristóbal Rovira Kaltwasser. Populism: A Very Short Introduction. Very Short Introductions. Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2017.

Articles

  • TBA

Games

  • ‘To build a better ballot: An interactive guide to alternative voting systems’. https://ncase.me/ballot
  • ‘District: A game about representation and redistricting’. https://polytrope.com/district
  • ‘Parable of the polygons: A playable post on the shape of society’. https://ncase.me/polygons
  • ‘The evolution of trust’. https://ncase.me/trust
  • ‘The wisdom and/or madness of crowds’. https://ncase.me/crowds
  • ‘We become what we behold: A game about news cycles, vicious cycles, infinite cycles’. https://ncase.itch.io/wbwwb

Podcasts

  • A. H. Halsey: Change in British Society. The Reith Lectures, 1978. (6 Episodes, 30 min.). https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p00gq29j
  • How Voters Decide. Analysis, BBC Radio 4, 2017. (2 Episodes, 30 min.). Part I: https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b08ff18d; Part II: https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b08g4m8w
  • Primate Politics. Analysis, BBC Radio 4, 2017. (30 min.). https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b09cympw
  • How identity politics elected Donald Trump. The Ezra Klein Show, 2018. (97 mins.). https://lnns.co/jWm-4DIxbSn
  • The Class Ceiling. Thinking Allowed, BBC Radio 4, 2019. (30 min.). https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/m000281t
